response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over 15 years of broad software development experience, open-source involvement, and genuine full-stack depth — but he falls critically short on the two most disqualifying dimensions for this role. First, the job requires minimum C1 German proficiency for client-facing consulting work in what is clearly a German-market firm; the resume explicitly lists German as 'Basic,' which is an outright blocker. Second, the role demands leadership of a team and client advisory responsibilities, yet there is no evidence of formal team management, project ownership, or client-facing technical advisory work — the closest analog is founding a side-project platform (GISCollective). Additionally, Python is absent from his skill set, UML is never mentioned, and there is no IT security or compliance exposure, all of which are listed requirements or strong preferences.
strengths: 15+ years of diverse, hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS domains — genuine technical breadth
Demonstrated open-source culture fit: maintains GISCollective, contributed D-language libraries, presented at FOSS4G, and set up self-hosted GitLab/CI infrastructure
Evidence of knowledge sharing and mentoring through recurring Refactoring Dojos, Code Katas, and multi-day public workshops — relevant to team lead responsibilities
concerns: German listed as 'Basic' — C1 is a stated hard requirement for this client-facing consulting role and cannot be bridged by English fluency alone
No evidence of formal team or project leadership: no direct reports managed, no project budget ownership, and no client advisory engagements described across the entire employment history
Python, UML, IT security/risk management, and compliance knowledge are all absent from the resume — these are explicitly required or desired by the job description

key_factors.factor: German language gap (Basic vs. C1 required)
